main_event: B-47 crew member observed elongated cylinder-shaped object with red core, white body and blue edges in shallow SW descent for approximately 3 seconds at 30,000 ft over Little Colorado River 15 miles south of Cameron, Arizona.
sighted_object: elongated cylinder; length approximately equal to size of baseball held at arms length; width approximately equal to size of half dollar held at arms length; core was red followed by white with blue edges; observer's attention drawn by a flash
witness_description: Captain, USAF, 340 Bomb Squadron, instructor pilot; reliability estimate Baker Two; aircraft tail number 53-1-854, magnetic heading 352 degrees, TAS 425 knots at 30,000 ft
interesting_points: Observer's attention drawn by a flash; could not determine if flash preceded object or was emitted by object
Object in shallow descent to SW, moving fast; lost sight as it passed below and behind aircraft
Position: 3541N 11119W (Little Colorado River, 15 mi S of Cameron AZ) at 30,000 ft, 14/0220Z
Intelligence officer noted observer's remarks concerning size indicated conscious definition of size at time of observation was suspect
PDF is a bundle: record card (p1) + unrelated Indiana Monon train FATE article and correspondence (p2-10) + Cameron teletype (p11-13) + unrelated Oregon/Salem/Pittsburgh/Indiana material (p14-18)
